text: Iliad (Troy) trailer made by Grok Imagine 1.5, which was just released https://t.co/o0zITVlvpn
summary: Iliad (Troy) trailer made by Grok Imagine 1.5, which was just released
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced the release of Grok Imagine 1.5, xAI's new image generation model, by sharing a trailer for the Iliad (Troy) created using this AI capability. This demonstrates xAI's progress in multimodal AI development and practical applications of their generative models, showing both technical advancement in AI/ML and a concrete product feature that could differentiate xAI's offerings in the competitive AI market.
embed_html:
Iliad (Troy) trailer made by Grok Imagine 1.5, which was just released pic.twitter.com/o0zITVlvpn

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 4, 2026

text:
Starlink V3 satellites have >10X bandwidth of V2 and there’ll be >10X launched, which means >100X more bandwidth. 

Also, altitude will be 350km vs 550km, so min latency can be cut in half. 

Light travels 300km/ms in space, so physics round trip min latency drops to <5ms.
summary: Starlink V3 satellites have >10X bandwidth of V2 and there’ll be >10X launched, which means >100X more bandwidth. Also, altitude will be 350km vs 550km, so min latency can be cut in half. Light travels 300km/ms in space, so physics round trip min latency drops to <5ms.
ai_summary: Elon Musk discusses Starlink V3 satellite specifications, highlighting a 100x bandwidth increase through both improved per-satellite capacity (10x) and deployment scale (10x more satellites), plus latency reduction to sub-5ms through lower orbital altitude (350km vs 550km). This represents critical product roadmap and technical performance milestones for Starlink's competitive positioning in global broadband infrastructure, directly impacting the company's ability to scale its satellite internet service and compete with traditional ISPs.
embed_html:

text:
29 more Starlink satellites. 

Over 10,000 in orbit now. https://t.co/QlP4LdWLPf
summary: 29 more Starlink satellites. Over 10,000 in orbit now.
ai_summary: Elon announces that SpaceX has deployed 29 additional Starlink satellites, bringing the total constellation to over 10,000 operational satellites in orbit. This milestone demonstrates significant progress in SpaceX's execution of their mega-constellation strategy for global broadband coverage, highlighting the operational cadence and scale of their satellite deployment operations—a critical metric for the company's path to profitability and market dominance in global internet infrastructure.
embed_html:
29 more Starlink satellites.

Over 10,000 in orbit now. https://t.co/QlP4LdWLPf

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 5, 2026

ai_summary: Elon Musk promotes Grok Voice, xAI's conversational AI product, by sharing a direct link for users to try it. This represents a product launch/availability update for Grok, xAI's AI assistant competing in the large language model space, demonstrating the company's expansion from text-based to voice-enabled AI capabilities.

text: @SawyerMerritt Something that perhaps isn’t clear is that, if this location works out (other locations are still in the running), SpaceX will still be paying an annual amount that increases tax revenue for Grimes County by ~25% and will be by far the biggest source of revenue for the county.

parent.embed_html:
Today, SpaceX received approval from Grimes County, Texas for huge tax breaks for the company's Terafab project.

The tax abatement would give SpaceX a full exemption on property taxes tied to the project.

County leaders have framed the Terafab proposal as a potentially… pic.twitter.com/O1rlehPRWA

— Sawyer Merritt (@SawyerMerritt) June 3, 2026

ai_summary: SpaceX secured tax incentives from Grimes County, Texas for its Terafab project, including property tax exemption. Elon clarifies that despite the tax break, SpaceX will increase the county's annual tax revenue by ~25% and become its largest revenue source, illustrating how major industrial projects can benefit local economies even with selective tax abatements. This demonstrates SpaceX's scale and the economic impact calculations involved in site selection and government negotiations for large manufacturing/infrastructure projects.
